title: Arrondissement of Mont-de-Marsan

infobox.value: is an [arrondissement](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_France "Arrondissements of France") of France in the [Landes](/wiki/Landes_(department) "Landes (department)") [department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France") in the [Nouvelle-Aquitaine](/wiki/Nouvelle-Aquitaine "Nouvelle-Aquitaine") [region](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France"). It has 175 [communes](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France").[[2]](#cite_note-insee2-2) Its population is 185,531 (2021), and its area is 6,048.4 km2 (2,335.3 sq mi).[[3]](#cite_note-insee-3) Its surface area of over 6,000 km2 is the largest of any arrondissement in [Metropolitan France](/wiki/Metropolitan_France "Metropolitan France"), its size being comparable to an entire département such as the [Eure](/wiki/Eure "Eure").

outgoing_links.target_title: Institut national de la statistique et des études économiques

Arrondissement in Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France

The **arrondissement of Mont-de-Marsan** is an [arrondissement](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_France "Arrondissements of France") of France in the [Landes](/wiki/Landes_(department) "Landes (department)") [department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France") in the [Nouvelle-Aquitaine](/wiki/Nouvelle-Aquitaine "Nouvelle-Aquitaine") [region](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France"). It has 175 [communes](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France").[[2]](#cite_note-insee2-2) Its population is 185,531 (2021), and its area is 6,048.4 km2 (2,335.3 sq mi).[[3]](#cite_note-insee-3) Its surface area of over 6,000 km2 is the largest of any arrondissement in [Metropolitan France](/wiki/Metropolitan_France "Metropolitan France"), its size being comparable to an entire département such as the [Eure](/wiki/Eure "Eure").

## Composition

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arrondissement_of_Mont-de-Marsan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Composition")]

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arrondissement_of_Mont-de-Marsan&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The arrondissement of Mont-de-Marsan was created in 1800.[[4]](#cite_note-splaf-4)

As a result of the reorganisation of the [cantons of France](/wiki/Cantons_of_France "Cantons of France") which came into effect in 2015, the borders of the cantons are no longer related to the borders of the arrondissements. The cantons of the arrondissement of Mont-de-Marsan were, as of January 2015:[[5]](#cite_note-insee2012-5)

1. Aire-sur-l'Adour
2. Gabarret
3. Geaune
4. Grenade-sur-l'Adour
5. Hagetmau
6. Labrit
7. Mimizan
8. Mont-de-Marsan-Nord
9. Mont-de-Marsan-Sud
10. Morcenx
11. Parentis-en-Born
12. Pissos
13. Roquefort
14. Sabres
15. Saint-Sever
16. Sore
17. Villeneuve-de-Marsan
